metadata.seed: I don’t want to be a burden on other people. So I never share anything and pretend I’m fine and smile.

patient.query: I don’t want to be a burden on other people. So I never share anything and pretend I’m fine and smile.

automatic_thoughts: I don’t want to be a burden on other people
I have to pretend I’m fine

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're really concerned about how others perceive you and whether you're causing them trouble. What makes you believe that sharing your experiences would be a burden? Let’s explore that a little further.

patient.query: I guess I've always felt like I should handle things on my own. It's like... I don’t want to drag anyone down with my problems. It just seems easier to hide what I’m going through. I worry that if I share too much, people will think I'm needy or just not handle it well.

automatic_thoughts: I should handle things on my own
I don’t want to drag anyone down with my problems
It seems easier to hide what I’m going through
If I share too much, people will think I'm needy
People will think I don't handle it well

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Fear of not coping with treatment (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you've developed a strong sense of responsibility to manage your difficulties independently. What makes it so important for you to handle things on your own, and what do you think might happen if you didn't?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it does feel like I have to be in control. I’ve always been that way. If I don’t handle it myself, I worry that I’d be a burden to others or that they’d see me as weak. I’ve seen people get frustrated when I tried to open up before, and that really sticks with me. It just makes me want to pull back even more

triggers: fear of being seen as weak
past experiences of frustration from others
automatic_thoughts: I have to be in control
if I don’t handle it myself, I’d be a burden
they’d see me as weak
people get frustrated when I open up

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like being in control has been a really important way for you to navigate relationships, and it’s understandable why you’d feel that way given what you’ve experienced. What do you think maintaining this sense of control has allowed you to do, and what might it be preventing?

patient.query: It’s definitely made me feel safer, like I can manage whatever comes my way. But I guess... it also keeps me isolated. I don’t let people in, and then I feel even more alone. Sometimes I think if I just let go a little, maybe things wouldn’t be so heavy, but it’s scary to think about. What if they don’t understand?

automatic_thoughts: if I just let go a little, maybe things wouldn’t be so heavy
what if they don’t understand

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you've found a way to feel in control, and that's valuable. What makes you think that letting go and allowing others in would be so difficult, or what's the worst thing that could happen if someone didn't understand?

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, it’s nice to feel in control, but I also hate feeling stuck. If I let someone in and they don’t get it, I guess... I’d feel vulnerable, like I’d opened up a part of myself that they could just dismiss. That thought makes me really anxious. It’s like, what if they just see me as messed up or weak? That just

automatic_thoughts: they don’t get it
I’d feel vulnerable
what if they just see me as messed up or weak

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Fear of losing control of bowels in public (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're caught between wanting to connect with someone and a worry about what might happen if you do. What makes you think they might dismiss a part of you if you were to open up?

patient.query: I think it comes from times when I’ve tried before and it didn’t go that well. Like, my friends didn’t know how to respond or they tried to fix things instead of just listening. It made me feel like my feelings were too much, like I was burdening them. So now, I just keep it all bottled up. It’s easier... or at least it feels that way

triggers: friends didn’t know how to respond
automatic_thoughts: my feelings were too much
I was burdening them
it’s easier to keep it all bottled up

neutral: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’ve learned to protect yourself from feeling misunderstood or like you’re asking too much of your friends. What makes you believe your feelings are too much for them to handle?

patient.query: I don't know... I guess I just feel like everyone has their own struggles, and my issues are just... heavy. I remember times when I felt like I was overwhelming someone, and it didn’t feel good. So, I think I just tell myself that I should be stronger, you know? If I could just deal with it, it wouldn’t be a problem for anyone else.

automatic_thoughts: I should be stronger
If I could just deal with it, it wouldn’t be a problem for anyone else

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Fear of not coping with treatment (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot, and it's understandable that you'd feel that way, especially when you've felt like you've been a burden in the past. What makes you believe that your struggles are heavier than what others are experiencing?

patient.query: I guess it just feels like everyone else has their things under control. I see them managing, and I think, “Why can’t I do that?” It’s like I should be able to handle it better. Maybe it’s just this fear that if I admit I’m struggling, people will see me differently, like I’m not capable or something. It’s hard to shake that feeling.

automatic_thoughts: Why can’t I do that?
I should be able to handle it better
If I admit I’m struggling, people will see me differently

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're holding yourself to a high standard, and it’s difficult when you feel like you’re not meeting it. What makes you believe that others have things completely under control, and what would it mean to you if that weren't true?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I just look at how people present themselves, and it seems like they have it all figured out. I see them smiling, pushing through life, and I can’t help but compare myself to that. If it turned out that everyone has their struggles... I’m not sure, honestly. It might feel a little less lonely, but it could also scare me. Thinking that we’re

automatic_thoughts: everyone has their struggles
it might feel a little less lonely
it could also scare me

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're holding onto a picture of how others appear, and it’s difficult to imagine them experiencing challenges. What makes it so unsettling to consider that others might be struggling too?

patient.query: It’s just... it makes me feel more vulnerable, I guess? If everyone is struggling, then we’re all in this messy situation together, and that’s... kind of overwhelming. I think I’d just be scared that if I opened up, I’d be seen as one more person trying to keep their head above water. It feels safer to think I'm the only one, like it separates me

it feels safer to think I'm the only one

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re trying to protect yourself from feeling overwhelmed, and that makes a lot of sense. What makes you think that sharing your experience would lead to others seeing you as just another person struggling?
